g.text: The mango sticky rice was unreal.
Temples, tea, and way too many
Shibuya crossing lives up to the hype.
Arrived early to beat the heat.
46m of pure gold. Got a traditional

Street food tour. Mango sticky rice,
Trip / Seoul / A week in Seoul
From palaces to pojangmacha — our 7 days in the capital.
Oct 28, 2026 · 8 min read · Seoul, South Korea
Seoul hit us like a gentle wave — unhurried, layered, and somehow
entirely familiar after only three days. We landed at Incheon on a
Tuesday morning with no real plan beyond "eat and walk a lot."
Gyeongbokgung opens at 9am and you should be there at 9am. We caught
the changing of the guard ceremony and spent nearly four hours just
wandering. Rented hanbok from a shop across the street — it's touristy
but you get free palace entry and the photos are worth it.
